D.Overdeveloped: Move aside. I will force the decision before the room drifts.
D.Healthy: Name the priority, assign the next step, and keep people with me.
D.Underdeveloped: If I cannot control it, I will detach and stop owning the outcome.

I.Overdeveloped: Watch me fix the energy before anyone notices the discomfort.
I.Healthy: Let me name the feeling, invite the room in, and keep the goal alive.
I.Underdeveloped: If the room does not respond, I will shrink, joke it off, or disappear.

S.Overdeveloped: Do not disrupt the room; I will carry the pressure so nobody else has to.
S.Healthy: Let us slow down, name what support is needed, and move steadily.
S.Underdeveloped: I will stay quiet, wait it out, and hope the pressure passes.

C.Overdeveloped: Stop. This is not accurate enough to move forward.
C.Healthy: Let us verify the next clean step and keep the system moving.
C.Underdeveloped: There is not enough clarity, so I will withhold until certainty appears.
